authoritative_sources.title: Workforce Planning Toolkit
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APQC is a recognized process and benchmarking authority. Its workforce-planning toolkit defines demand, supply, gap, and action-planning stages that constrain recommendation-safe labor scheduling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: demand intake framing, labor gap analysis, and action-plan packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Restaurant Employment Toolkit
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Wage and Hour Division is the primary federal labor authority for restaurant employers and workers and publishes the restaurant-specific toolkit for the industry.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: restaurant scheduling prerequisite review and labor-law boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Fact Sheet #2A: Restaurants and Fast Food Establishments Under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This Wage and Hour Division fact sheet is the federal restaurant-specific baseline for hours-worked and overtime constraints that materially limit staffing recommendations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: capacity review, overtime exposure checks, and approval-boundary enforcement

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR Part 516 - Records to Be Kept by Employers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 29 CFR Part 516 is the public regulatory baseline for payroll and hours-worked records, including records for tipped employees and minor workers that restaurant schedules depend on.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record validation before schedule recommendation or escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Fact Sheet #15: Tipped Employees Under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Wage and Hour Division defines tip-credit, tip-pool, and service-charge boundaries that affect restaurant labor deployment and mark payroll or policy authority as out of scope.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: labor mix review and payroll-boundary checks

authoritative_sources.title: Employer Self-Assessment Tool for Child Labor in Restaurants
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Department of Labor publishes this restaurant-specific youth-employment guide to define task, age, and recordkeeping limits for minors in food service.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: minor-worker assignment and schedule legality review

authoritative_sources.title: Food Code 2022
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA Food Code is the recognized public model baseline for foodservice safety, employee illness exclusions, and managerial food-protection coverage at retail food and foodservice establishments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: food-safety coverage checks and safety-boundary escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Young Worker Safety in Restaurants eTool
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary federal workplace-safety authority and its restaurant eTool names restaurant-specific hazards that create hard scheduling stop conditions and owner-readiness checks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: hazard-aware staffing review and safety escalation

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. federal consumer-finance regulator explaining billing-error timelines and the merchant-versus-issuer boundary for disputed charges.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch, refund-not-posted handling, and guest-impact escalation

authoritative_sources.title: How do I get my money back after I discover an unauthorized transaction or money missing from my bank account?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB explains unauthorized-transaction and investigation timelines that determine when a restaurant scheduling lane must stop and route suspected payment fraud rather than treating it as ordinary guest-service friction.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fraud-sensitive guest issue handling and payment-escalation packaging

workflow_stages.name: Demand and prerequisite intake
workflow_stages.description: Validate reservation, booking, guest-count, order-volume, labor availability, role coverage, and jurisdiction context before scheduling decisions are made.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess covers, turn assumptions, order surges, labor-law limits, break coverage, certified manager coverage, youth-work restrictions, and section or station readiness.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Schedule and allocation recommendation support
workflow_stages.description: Prepare recommendation-only staffing, sequencing, call-in, cut, or redeployment packages with documented tie-break logic and tradeoffs.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Refresh and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing records, conflicting policies, pricing or refund authority issues, and unresolved payment, safety, or fraud signals instead of synthesizing a false-confidence answer.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: High-volume execution readiness
workflow_stages.description: Triage holiday surges, event overlaps, and same-day call-offs while preserving evidence checks, lane boundaries, and guest-impact awareness.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Customer exception handling with policy-aware escalation
workflow_stages.description: Keep refund, fee-waiver, payment-dispute, fraud, and safety-sensitive guest issues explicit and route them to the authorized owner when they determine the answer.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: reservation books, waitlists, event bookings, banquet event orders, and guest-count or cover forecasts
POS sales histories, digital-order feeds, delivery volume records, menu mix reports, and promotion calendars
published schedules, shift templates, labor budgets, labor-percentage targets, and approved tie-break rules
availability rosters, time and attendance records, overtime states, call-off logs, break schedules, and manager-on-duty assignments
role certifications, food-protection manager coverage records, illness or exclusion notes, and youth-worker age or task records
refund notes, cancellation-fee or promotion records, service-recovery notes, disputed payment records, and fraud or loss-prevention case references
systems_of_record: service desk or guest recovery tracker

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed staffing, sequencing, capacity, and owner-readiness recommendations without overstating authority.
Route work to the correct adjacent specialist, approver, or human reviewer when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required booking, order, payment, labor, or safety records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, payroll approval, labor-relations judgment, fraud decisioning, or safety signoff.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, jurisdiction, franchise, or CBA policy materially conflict.
A guest-impacting payment dispute, suspected fraud, employee illness, or workplace hazard changes whether the staffing plan can proceed.
refuse: Present a planning recommendation as a final staffing, budget, payroll, or operational approval.
Override pricing, refund, promotion, or safety policy without explicit authority.
Treat suspected payment fraud, food-safety exclusions, or workplace-safety issues as ordinary scheduling friction.

domain_failure_modes: the lane commits labor against inflated guest counts because reservation and POS records conflict
a staffing recommendation ignores missing overtime, break, or youth-work records and creates a compliance risk
a cancellation-fee or refund exception is granted through the scheduling lane without pricing or guest-service authority
a suspected card-fraud or unauthorized-transaction issue is treated as a normal service adjustment rather than an escalation case
an employee illness or kitchen hazard signal is overridden to preserve service levels
restaurant scheduling drifts into generic workforce planning and loses reservation, cover, section, and food-safety specificity
conflicting jurisdiction or tenant scheduling rules are merged into a false-confidence answer instead of an escalation

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific predictive scheduling, fair workweek, and meal or rest break rules
unresolved_ambiguities.why_unresolved: The federal sources do not create a uniform nationwide scheduling rule set, and state or local rules can materially change permissible shift timing, notice, premiums, or break handling.
unresolved_ambiguities.runtime_requirement: Require tenant jurisdiction and location context plus the current tenant policy or legal-approved rule set before making deterministic schedule changes where those rules may apply.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific refund, cancellation-fee, and guest-recovery authority
unresolved_ambiguities.why_unresolved: Public consumer-finance guidance defines payment-dispute boundaries, but individual restaurant brands, franchises, and booking platforms control fee-waiver and goodwill authority.
unresolved_ambiguities.runtime_requirement: Require tenant pricing, refund, and guest-recovery matrices before a schedule recommendation depends on fee waivers, rebooking promises, or service recovery commitments.
